# The recent advances in vaccine adjuvants

### Affiliation

# The recent advances in vaccine adjuvants

### Authors

### Affiliation

## Abstract

Vaccine adjuvants, as key components in enhancing vaccine immunogenicity, play a vital role in modern vaccinology. This review systematically examines the historical evolution and mechanisms of vaccine adjuvants, with particular emphasis on innovative advancements in aluminum-based adjuvants, emulsion-based adjuvants, and nucleic acid adjuvants (e.g., CpG oligonucleotides). Specifically, aluminum adjuvants enhance immune responses through particle formation/antigen adsorption, inflammatory cascade activation, and T-cell stimulation. Emulsion adjuvants amplify immunogenicity via antigen depot effects and localized inflammation, while nucleic acid adjuvants like CpG oligonucleotides directly activate B cells and dendritic cells to promote Th1-type immune responses and memory T-cell generation. The article further explores the prospective applications of these novel adjuvants in combating emerging pathogens (including influenza and SARS-CoV-2), particularly highlighting their significance in improving vaccine potency and durability. Moreover, this review underscores the critical importance of adjuvant development in next-generation vaccine design and provides theoretical foundations for creating safer, effective adjuvant.

**Keywords:**
adjuvants; combinatorial adjuvant strategies; delivery systems; immunostimulants; vaccine.

Copyright © 2025 Xing, Zhao, Li, Fang, Sun, Zhang and Song.

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://www.ims.u-tokyo.ac.jp/imsut/content/000003596.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A diagram illustrating the process of vaccine development against a virus, highlighting the roles of live-attenuated and inactivated vaccines, dendritic cells, T cells, B cells, and the use of an adjuvant to enhance immune response, with a specific focus on molecule 6 (cholicamide).

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://pub.mdpi-res.com/vaccines/vaccines-11-01172/article_deploy/html/images/vaccines-11-01172-ag-550.jpg?1688025243
DESCRIPTION: The illustration presents various nano-based adjuvants and carriers, including nano-alums, nano-clays, and nano-emulsions, alongside virus-based nanocarriers like viral vectors, virus-like particles, and virosomes, all connected to the concept of nano-based vaccines.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://pub.mdpi-res.com/vaccines/vaccines-11-01172/article_deploy/html/images/vaccines-11-01172-g003.png?1688025238
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the mechanisms of adjuvant technology in vaccines, showing different vaccine types like Adv, AAV, rVSV, Lentiviral vectors, and VLPs, and their role in stimulating humoral and cellular immunity through antigen presentation and T cell activation.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://www.croda.com/mediaassets/images/health-care/health-care-new-website/product-ranges-and-brands/adjuvants/vaccine-adjuvants.jpg?w=1920&la=en-GB&focalpointcrop=1&xratio=0&yratio=0&hash=5BB139568CFC411BB26BB42EB3A1F64B
DESCRIPTION: A digital illustration of a syringe composed of interconnected glowing blue lines and particles, surrounded by abstract representations of antibodies and molecular structures, highlighting innovations in adjuvant technology for vaccines.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://pub.mdpi-res.com/bioengineering/bioengineering-08-00155/article_deploy/html/images/bioengineering-08-00155-ag.png?1635069969
DESCRIPTION: A diagram illustrating the latest adjuvant technology for vaccines, with different adjuvants enhancing immune responses to a new pandemic antigen, leading to various vaccine formulations.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Recent advancements include nanodelivery systems and novel formulations, enhancing the efficacy and safety of vaccine responses, as highlighted in emerging research on adjuvants.
Tailored adjuvant strategies are being developed to optimize immune responses based on individual genetic profiles, facilitating more effective and targeted vaccines for diverse populations.

enriched_points.point_title: Improving vaccine responses in vulnerable populations
enriched_points.expanded_content: Innovative adjuvants aim to bolster immune responses in at-risk groups, such as the elderly and immunocompromised, ensuring equitable access to effective vaccination.
The integration of advanced adjuvant technologies may pave the way for novel vaccine platforms, including DNA and mRNA vaccines, revolutionizing immunization approaches.
Despite progress, challenges persist in standardizing adjuvant formulations and ensuring safety across diverse populations, necessitating ongoing research and regulatory evaluations.
